behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is usually at module handoff (history builder/prompt assembler/LLM client): trimming logic may exist but the wrong slice/order can still be sent downstream. A pure unit test of the trim helper can miss that wiring failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the chatbot request path with a 15-message history against a test double LLM client that records the actual payload. Assert exactly the latest 10 messages (correct order and roles) are sent, and older messages are excluded.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model semantic quality. Deterministic mocks cannot reveal off-topic but syntactically valid replies; this is a model-judgment failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative customer questions to the real LLM-backed response path and evaluate relevance (rubric or human-labeled checks) over repeated runs/sampled prompts. Fail if responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Whether a response is 'too formal' is model-dependent classification quality, not just wiring. Mocking tone-check logic would likely hide real misclassification failures.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier conversations that commonly induce formal drafts, run with real tone-check model behavior, and assert formal drafts are flagged and a regeneration call is issued. Track trigger rate against labeled expectations.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is local service/repository/database wiring and state persistence. In-process mocks can miss schema/transaction/timestamp mapping issues that appear with a real test DB.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chatbot flows (with and without regeneration) against a real local test database. Assert stored records include user message, first draft (if generated), regenerated response, linkage fields, and valid monotonic timestamps.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is orchestration/state handoff between generation, tone-check, and response assembly. A unit test of one function can miss leakage of the first draft through controller/service boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the end-to-end local request path with deterministic LLM stubs that force regeneration. Assert outbound API payload contains only the regenerated response, and rejected draft is absent from customer-visible fields while still logged internally as needed.
